originalAlarm.description: Memory dynamically allocated by `malloc`, indirectly via call to `extcon_class_init()` on line 107 is not freed after the last access at line 112, column 1.

presenceCondition: (and (= CONFIG_ANDROID true) (= CONFIG_SYSFS true) (= CONFIG_EXTCON true))
model:
(define-fun CONFIG_SYSFS () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_ANDROID () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_EXTCON () Bool
  true)
reasoning: The known VBDB bug for 0dc77b6dabe.c is at L37 (sysfs_link_sibling called a second time returns error because linked\!=NULL). This alarm detects a memory leak: extcon_class_init() at L107 allocates kobj via class_compat_register; extcon_class_exit() at L108 does nothing (bug: should free); extcon_class_init() at L109 allocates a new kobj and stores it in switch_class, overwriting and leaking the first. New bug. ANDROID=T internally #defines CONFIG_SYSFS (L2), making SYSFS=T in the PC redundant but consistent (not contradictory). The bug requires ANDROID=T (for switch_class to exist and class_compat_register to be called) and EXTCON=T (for extcon functions to be compiled). The PC {ANDROID=T, SYSFS=T, EXTCON=T} correctly captures the bug condition. Correctly constrained.
